title: Four decisions, four traps
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: Three of the four lines are wrong. The first claims the stock was never counted — and it is zero.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
hint2.changed-data: You can change it while testing, but put it back at the end.
why.loose-left: Somewhere you are still comparing with two signs.
hint2.loose-left: The number of cups arrives as text. Convert it before comparing it with a number.
why.not-joined: The tiers are still not joined.
hint2.not-joined: Two separate checks both pass. They should be one decision, with the highest tier at the top.
hint3.not-joined: Add the case for under ten lev too — tier None.
why.no-or: Priority is still two separate checks.
hint2.no-or: One or the other is written with two vertical bars.
why.wrong-stock: The stock is reported as missing.
hint2.wrong-stock: Zero cups is a known fact. A condition with just a name asks whether it is truthy, and zero is not.
hint2.wrong-order: Two cups were typed. With three signs the text "2" is not the number 2.
hint2.wrong-tier: Thirty lev passes both conditions. The right answer comes out by accident, because Silver is written at the bottom.
hint2.wrong-priority: The customer is not a member and the total is thirty, not fifty.
why.wrong-count: There are not four lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Four decisions on one order. Nothing breaks, everything runs from start to finish.
blocks.1.text: Three of the four answers are wrong. The fourth is right by accident and will become wrong the moment somebody moves two lines.
blocks.2.text: What it is supposed to do
blocks.3.text: Stock of zero cups, two ordered, a customer with no membership, a total of thirty lev.
blocks.4.items.0: the stock is known and it is zero
blocks.5.text: The right answer is not proof
blocks.6.text: The tier comes out Silver and that is correct. But not because the code is right — because Silver is written below Bronze and overwrites it.
blocks.7.text: Swap the two blocks and the same code gives Bronze for thirty lev. Nothing changes in the logic — only the order.
blocks.8.quote: Code whose correctness depends on the order of its lines is not correct code yet.
blocks.10.text: Fix the four decisions. Do not touch the data.
blocks.11.text: Each broken thing comes from a lesson in this section. If you cannot place one, the hint ladder is under every failed check.
